programmes.show_description: Boxer Willie Reynolds is coming back but has lost his edge - Terry stops minding him and ends up as his trainer, but the promoter isn't pleased by this. S2 E4: Don't Tell Them Willie Boy Was Here
Alf loses his job and comes home to find he may have been burgled. It's an unhappy birthday but a surprise is in store. S7 E6: Unemployment
The Ropers are invited for a meal not realising that Robin has invited his German friend Franz; the meal does not go well as George has a grudge against Germans. S2 E4: Did You Ever Meet Rommel?
Linda is delighted when her heartthrob - soap actor Frazer Hines - books into the hotel, but he only has eyes for Amy. S2 E4: El Astro

Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S4, E4: When Love Walks In

All along the route of the United Kingdom's 'Premier Line' the West Coast Main Line, we find numerous small 'Byways of Steam' that the local railways built to serve the small villages and hamlets.

Arthur lines up a deal to supply cheap tobacco, but Terry has his doubts when he and Arthur are on a boat being pursued by Customs and Excise. S5 E1: Goodbye Sailor
An Irishman attempts to bribe Lady Marjorie with love letters she had once written; Thomas gets wrapped up in the web of deceit, but will money get the better of him? S2 E6: The Property of a Lady
Grandad has a fall and Adrian takes up art in a big way. While Aveline has her first quarrel with her fiance, Shifty has a date. S5 E3
Harold is finally tying the knot but is jilted at the altar, leaving the bridegroom humiliated and in a terrible state; however, the best man is far from upset. S4 E1: And Afterwards At...
The station is prepared for a visit by the chairman of British Railways, but it turns out to be a different Doctor Beeching altogether. S2 E8: Action Stations
Rigsby is ecstatic to learn that he has inherited his Uncle George's fortune but distressed at a clause that he must be happily married. S4 E3: Great Expectations
The youngsters want a party, but George - recalling the last one - is against the idea. Mildred, however, is all for it and gives it her blessing... S6 E1: The Party's Over
Gary has become overconfident about his relationships with both Phoebe and Yvonne, and boasts to Ron about his prowess with women. S3 E5: Change Partners
Bella takes in a young boy whose mother has died; when he steals 10 shillings, Father Keenley arranges for the boy to be sent on an emigration scheme to Australia. S1 E2: Say Hello...Say Tirra

John Sullivan's bittersweet sitcom. Vince traces Penny's address and decides to pay a visit, but her mother has not forgiven him. S1 E2: Hello Again
Robin has failed his cookery exam and considers moving back to Southampton, but the girls are far from pleased at the thought of his mate Larry moving in. S2 E6: Carry Me Back to Old Southampton
Sitcom starring Michael Crawford. Convinced he is a failure, Frank visits a psychiatrist. S1 E6: The Psychiatrist
Sharon and Tracey discover that Dorien has been keeping a secret for over fifty years and decide to get involved, but Dorien is reluctant to confront her past. S11 E3: Guess Who's Coming to Essex?

Gary arouses suspicions when he saves Phoebe from an early evening bombing raid. S1 E3: Is Your Journey Really Necessary?
There's been a spare bed in Philip's room since Alan moved out, and Rigsby has no intentions of keeping it empty. S4 E2: Fire And Brimstone
Bob thinks that Terry needs new friends, but inviting him to Alan and Brenda's trendy dinner party might not be such a great idea. S1 E8: Guess Who's Coming to Dinner?
When reformed Ron the Burglar manages to open Arthur's lock-up, Arthur realises it's time for Daley Enterprises to strengthen its warehouse security. S8 E8: Him Indoors
Solana Group CEO Crystal Hennessy-Vass sweeps into the resort and takes Joyce out to lunch to announce that the staff budget is being cut by half - and Joyce is being replaced. S7 E5
Ian is assaulted, and left confused about his attacker's motive. When they strike again, Ian is hospitalised - and there's more bad news when he regains consciousness: he's got amnesia. S6 E1
Robin has failed his cookery exam and considers moving back to Southampton, but the girls are far from pleased at the thought of his mate Larry moving in. S2 E6: Carry Me Back to Old Southampton
Rigsby attempts to have himself cast as male lead in a play written by effeminate lodger Hilary when he discovers that Ruth is playing the role of the love interest. S3 E2: Stage Struck
Solana Group CEO Crystal Hennessy-Vass sweeps into the resort and takes Joyce out to lunch to announce that the staff budget is being cut by half - and Joyce is being replaced. S7 E5
When he arrives home to find a nine-year-old boy on his doorstep, Terry takes on a minding job he hadn't anticipated and later teaches someone a lesson. S2 E5: Not a Bad Lad, Dad
The story of bad tempered Alf Garnett who has not mellowed with the years and is as bigoted as ever. S1 E1
Jo is going on a date with Philip but has forgotten that she is meant to be babysitting; Chrissy agrees to take her place and persuades Robin to come with her. S2 E5: Two Foot Two, Eyes Of Blue
Returning from a boat trip to Tangier, Amy is approached by a Spaniard who asks her to deliver a box allegedly containing the ashes of his dead friend Pepe to his widowed mother. S2 E5: Pepe

Dave at the Winchester Arms points out that banknotes given to Arthur by Shamy to pay off his debts have been forged. S5 E2: What Makes Shamy Run?
Having brothers visit them in London causes both Richard and Hudson complications and strained relations, but will the repercussions be worse for Hudson? S2 E7: Your Obedient Servant
Mrs Boswell bonds with new neighbour Celia, and Billy manages to upset Adrian in the middle of a poetry recital in the kitchen. S5 E4
Harold picks up a rare antique figurine on his rounds bearing the well-known crossed swords; thinking he's found an item worth selling, he is determined to make a large profit. S4 E2: Crossed Swords
It's Jack and May's wedding anniversary, but May is worried that she might be pregnant - meanwhile, Arnold fears that he is about to get the sack. S2 E9: A Pregnant Pause
Rigsby decides to move on, give up on Miss Jones and file for divorce - he places an advert in the local newspaper, but his search for love doesn't run entirely smoothly. S4 E4: Pink Carnations
When the lease is up on the flat and George wants to increase the rent, the flatmates decide that the only way to raise the money is to get a fourth tenant to share. S6 E2: One More for the Pot
Ron unexpectedly turns up at the Sparrow household with suitcase in hand. Phoebe suggests Gary should assume teaching duties for the local children. S3 E6: Goodnight Children Everywhere

Times are hard as the miners strike; Jack has money and is now engaged to Jessie, but Tom threatens to expose him when he sleeps with the widowed sister of a war comrade. S1 E3: Fish in Woolly Jumpers

John Sullivan's bittersweet sitcom. Penny and Vince test their feelings for each other over dinner at Vince's flat. S1 E3: Let's Spend the Night Together
Larry is staying with Robin having been thrown out of his flat, but Larry moves in when the Ropers decide to convert the attic into another flat. S3 E1: Cuckoo in the Nest
Sitcom starring Michael Crawford. The new manager of a labour exchange refuses to believe that Frank is unemployable. S1 E7: The Employment Exchange
Tracey gets a letter from her auntie Vera who is retiring as a Pearly Queen and wants her to take over, but Tracey faces competition from a jealous Sharon. S11 E4: The Girls With The Pearl Buttons

Terry helps Bob move his most treasured possessions from his old house to the new one, but Thelma refuses to have his junk in their home. S1 E9: Storm in a Tea Chest
Arthur plans to boost the membership of the Winchester Club, but ends up in trouble over a stolen karaoke machine. S8 E9: The Greatest Show in Willesden
Joyce tries to relax by the pool, but her morning is ruined when Janey reinstates Jason. Clive is swindled out of 5,000 euros. Tiger cannot take his eyes off Annie Redmond. S7 E6
With the launch night of the newly redecorated Jockey looming, Jamie and Karen are feeling the pressure in their relationship. Can a lesbian encounter put the spice back into their marriage? S6 E2

Rigsby turns up at the house with a new sports car apparently purchased in yet another attempt to woo Miss Jones, but his driving doesn't appear to be as stylish as his motor. S3 E3: Clunk Click
